title: phyllanthus gordonii
intro: Phyllanthus gordonii, also known as Guduchi or Amrita, is a plant native to tropical regions of Asia and Africa, belonging to the family Phyllanthaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, improving liver function, and boosting the immune system. Phyllanthus gordonii can be prepared in herbal teas, capsules, or supplements, and is also used in traditional Indian medicine to create formulations such as ghee and oil infusions.
